comment.body:
Core CPI (no food and energy prices) is expected to grow a bit faster than the consensus forecast of 0.3%, leading to a YoY rate of about 3.19%,
and 0.36% headline increase MoM:
But don't forget!! Fed watches the core CPI, thats what they use in real. 

HOWEVER, the increase in CPI is expected due to the increased demand for used cars, and car insurance as insurers pass on higher repair, car, and litigation costs to consumers.
+ postage price increase (mostly due to the higher activity again, seasonal thing).
So, nothing serious, and when it will be revised, using weights to the spendings on different sectors, it is expected to be ok.

market expects monthly inflation to moderate to around 0.25% in the coming months. the longer-term outlook points toward a YoY core CPI of about 2.8%

For now the consensus is 0.34% MoM in headline.

comment.body:
bottom Line
40% chance of a neutral outcome (~6098–6129 SPX range).
30% chance of an upside move (6129–6174 SPX).
30% chance of a downside move (5977–6022 SPX).
JPM’s projections suggest more market sensitivity to higher CPI prints than GS’s estimates.

Takeaway: CPI near 0.28%–0.33% is the most likely outcome (~40% probability), keeping markets stable. However, a surprise above 0.34% or below 0.20% could cause significant volatility.
   GS and JPM 'combined'  JPM is more bearish'
